results.taskDescription: Fetch top 15 HN posts and write a paragraph-style summary categorizing them by topic. Tests substantive synthesis from observation data.
results.output:
# Today on Hacker News
## Technology & Software
The technology sector is currently dominated by several key developments. Notably, Valve has released the Steam Controller CAD files under a Creative Commons license, allowing enthusiasts to modify or create custom controllers for their gaming setups. In software development circles, RaTeX emerges as an interesting project—a KaTeX-compatible LaTeX rendering engine written entirely in Rust, promising faster and more efficient mathematical typesetting. Additionally, Grand Theft Oil Futures has caught attention with its critical analysis of insider trading in oil futures markets.

## Open Source & Hardware
The open source community is seeing a resurgence with Agent-harness-kit, an MCP provider-agnostic scaffolding for multi-agent workflows, and SQLite being recognized as a Library of Congress Recommended Storage Format. On the hardware side, Indian matchbox labels are emerging as a unique visual archive, showcasing the country's graphic design history.

## Workplace & Personal Development
Workplace productivity strategies take center stage with an insightful article on 'Appearing Productive in the Workplace,' offering practical tips for professionals seeking to enhance their visibility and effectiveness. Meanwhile, LinkedIn is facing scrutiny over its visitor list policies as Noyb advocates for user data ownership rights.

## Miscellaneous
A variety of other topics cover everything from permacomputing principles and diskless Linux boot configurations using ZFS/iSCSI/PXE, to the adventurous tale of brave individuals who purchased a used rental camper van with over 340k miles. The list also includes an intriguing project titled 'SingleRide' which explores finding the longest route in NYC's subway without repeating stations.

This compilation highlights the diverse range of discussions on Hacker News, from technological innovations to societal concerns and personal development strategies.
